issue_title: Keycloak throws a 500 when invalid Accept header
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

If you perform a request like the following:

```
curl -H "accept: x;/x" -d "" http://mykeycloak.com/realms/my-realm/login-actions/authenticate
```

Keycloak responds with a 500 instead of a proper status code. This happens in the last version 26.4.7 and also on 26.3.x. I am not sure about older versions. This also happens to other headers like `Content-Type`.

Stacktrace:
```
2025-12-05 14:45:48,414 ERROR [io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.QuarkusErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) HTTP Request to /realms/my-realm/login-actions/authenticate failed, error id: 0e4235fa-3831-46dc-a033-af32e73c6a29-1: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Failed to parse media type x;/x
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.headers.MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.internalParse(MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.java:84)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.headers.MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.parse(MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.java:53)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.headers.MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.fromString(MediaTypeHeaderDelegate.java:128)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.util.MediaTypeHelper.valueOf(MediaTypeHelper.java:27)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.headers.HeaderUtil.getAcceptableMediaTypes(HeaderUtil.java:287)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.HttpHeadersImpl.getModifiableAcceptableMediaTypes(HttpHeadersImpl.java:103)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.HttpHeadersImpl.getAcceptableMediaTypes(HttpHeadersImpl.java:95)
at org.keycloak.utils.MediaTypeMatcher.isAcceptMediaType(MediaTypeMatcher.java:17)
at org.keycloak.utils.MediaTypeMatcher.isHtmlRequest(MediaTypeMatcher.java:9)
at org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler.getResponse(KeycloakErrorHandler.java:91)
at org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler.toResponse(KeycloakErrorHandler.java:73)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.core.RuntimeExceptionMapper.mapException(RuntimeExceptionMapper.java:96)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.core.ResteasyReactiveRequestContext.mapExceptionIfPresent(ResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:408)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.ExceptionHandler.handle(ExceptionHandler.java:15)
at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:192)
at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$15.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:645)
at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2651)
at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2630)
at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor.runThreadBody(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1622)
at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1589)
at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
```

### Version

26.4.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It should return a proper HTTP status code, like 415 Unsupported Media Type. Otherwise it can cause denial of service.

### Actual behavior

A 500 internal server error is returned

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak and perform the following request:
```
curl -H "accept: x;/x" -d "" http://mykeycloak.com/realms/my-realm/login-actions/authenticate
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

fix: preventing raw stacktrace response and error log (#44815)

closes: #44712

issue_title: No "Sign in with Passkey" on first step with organization:<alias> scope
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

When providing the `organization:<alias>` scope, a passkey can not be used on the first login page. You are forced to first enter an email address (of an existing account). Only on the password form can you then use the passkey.

Any other variation of the organization scope allows a passkey to be used on the first page.

|Organization Scope|Initial Login page|
|---|---|
|none|Email or passkey|
|`organization`|Email or passkey|
|`organization:*`|Email  or passkey|
|`organization:<alias>`|Email|

### Version

26.4.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

You can log in using a passkey without first entering your email address when using the `organization:<alias>` scope.

### Actual behavior

"Sign in with passkey" is not available on the first page of the login flow when using the `organization:<alias>` scope.

### How to Reproduce?

- Organizations enabled and at least one organization created
- Passkeys enabled 
- Use Built-in browser flow (from 26.4)
- (Email as Username enabled)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CredentialRequest requires that client scope is assigned as 'Optional'
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Let's assume that I send credential request for the client scope requesting credential related to the client scope `sd-jwt-credential` . As long as client scope is assigned to the client as "Optional" client scope, this works OK. However if the client scope is assigned to the client as "Default" client scope, it doesn't work.

Hint: As of today, there are some places in the `OID4VCIssuerEndpoint` class calling something like : `ClientScopeModel clientScope = clientModel.getClientScopes(false)...` 

This considers only "optional" client scopes, but not "default" client scopes assigned to the client.

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-08)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

All client scopes assigned to the client should be considered during credentialRequest

### Actual behavior

Only "optional" client scopes assigned to the client are considered during credentialRequest

### How to Reproduce?

See details.

### Anything else?

_No response_

[OID4VCI] Fix OID4VCI credential requests to restrict Default client scopes (#45011)

Closes #44737

issue_title: Wrong keycloak session when restarting workflows
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When restarting the workflow, the session associated with the execution context is the session from where the task was created, and not the session when executing the task.

This causes issues where the original session is already closed when executing the steps in a workflow after a restart.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The session created during the task execution should be used.

### Actual behavior

The session that created the task is used during the task execution.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a workflow with a `restart` step.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OID4VC metadata endpoint returns deferred_credential_endpoint even if not supported
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

Keycloak OID4VC Issuer endpoint returns in the metadata the `deferred_credential_endpoint` with value similar like `https://as.keycloak-fapi.org:8443/realms/test/protocol/oid4vc/deferred_credential` . However Keycloak does not have support for the deferred credential endpoint yet. Considering this, it cane be good to either remove it from the metadata or add support for that endpoint.

For the bugfix, removing from metadata is probably sufficient. Adding proper support for this endpoint might be a follow-up (However not sure yet how it should work. Maybe email/notification should be sent to the admin that user requested the credential and leaving to the administrator to do something to acomplish it. Maybe integration with workflows feature...?)

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-09)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Deferred-credential endpoint not being returned in the OID4VCI metadata, or make sure to support it (which might be more tricky task...)

### Actual behavior

Deferred-credential endpoint returned in the OID4VCI metadata even if unsupported

### How to Reproduce?

- Start Keycloak with `oid4vc-vci` feature supported.
- Login to admin console
- Enable `Verifiable credential` switch for some realm
- Open the metadata endpoint like https://as.keycloak-fapi.org:8443/.well-known/openid-credential-issuer/realms/test and seeing the response

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove deferred credential endpoint from OID4VC metadata (#44907)

Closes #44779

issue_title: link to reset-credentials ignores default locale AND ui_locales
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

In our realm, the enabled locales are English and Dutch, and the latter is the default locale.
For some pages, such as the default login page, the default language isn't used, but we could fix those by adding URL parameter `ui_locales=nl`. That fix does not work for this issue, though.



### Version

26.3.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When you link straight to a required-action page of the login flow, such as reset credentials, it should choose choose the realm's default locale, or at least choose the locale in an URL parameter such as `ui_locales=nl`.

### Actual behavior

The reset credentials page "required action" page gets shown in English by default, even after adding a query parameter like `ui_locales=nl` or `kc_locale=nl`, it still shows English by default.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set realm's locales to English and Dutch
2. Set realm's default locale to Dutch
3. Try to open the reset-credentials page directly, including (or excluding) a `ui_locales=nl` parameter: `https://the-server.nl/realms/the-realm/login-actions/reset-credentials?client_id=some_client&ui_locales=nl`

### Anything else?

I assume this bug extends to other `/login-actions` pages, but we have not tested those.
URL parameter `kc_locale=nl` also didn't work.
commit_message:
support locale in reset-credentials direct link

closes #44784

issue_title: Can not get through SSO login if using a custom attribute with default value
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

If you define a custom attribute, that the user can not edit himself and also give it a default value, then the user can't login via SSO. He always lands on the Review Profile Page and can't get any further.

### Version

26.4.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When I set a custom attribute with default value, I expect it to be set when user is created via SSO without interference and have the defaul value.

### Actual behavior

Users can not get through the login process anymore if default is set.

### How to Reproduce?

* Configure a realm-attribute like this:

```json
 {
    "name": "usertype",
    "displayName": "Usertype",
    "permissions": {
      "edit": [
        "admin"
      ],
      "view": [
        "admin",
        "user"
      ]
    },
    "multivalued": false,
    "annotations": {
      "inputType": "select-radiobuttons"
    },
    "validations": {
      "options": {
        "min": "",
        "max": "",
        "options": [
          "daily",
          "power"
        ]
      }
    },
    "defaultValue": "daily"
}
```

* Setup an OIDC Identity Provider at will with first-broker-flow.
* Login via SSO.
* You now see the /realms/.../login-actions/required-action?execution=VERIFY_PROFILE Page and can't do anything, since users are not allowed to edit the attribute and the default is not respected during user-creation.


### Anything else?

The following changes are workarounds, but don't fullfill the intended use:

* Leave the default blank and handle the value being undefined in Application itself
* Allow user to edit the attribute (Then the VERIFY_PROFILE page does not show up)
* Add a hardcoded mapper to the Identity Provider setting the attribute.
commit_message:
Can not get through SSO login if using a custom attribute with default value

Closes #44785

Signed-off-by: Martin Kanis <mkanis@redhat.com>

issue_title: CredentialRequest with credentialIdentifier does not work when credential-offer was created by OAuth2 authorization_code grant
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

In case that there is credentialRequest sent to the OID4VCI credential endpoint with the `credential_identifier` parameter, which relates to the parameter returned in the OAuth2 token response (in case of OAuth2 `authorization_code` grant), then the request would fail with the error like:

This update https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44642 broke this functionality as now stored credentialOffer is supposed to store the `user_id` , however during creation of credential-offer in case of OAuth2 authorization_code grant ( done in `OID4VCAuthorizationDetailsProcessor.process`) , there is still `username` stored inside the credential-offer instead of user ID

### Expected behavior

Credential request should work when sent with `credential_identifier` and credential-offer was created by OAuth2 authz code flow

### Actual behavior

Credential request fails when sent with `credential_identifier` and credential-offer was created by OAuth2 authz code flow

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Claims configued by OID4VCI protocol mappers as mandatory are not enforced to be mandatory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

ATM When there is OID4VCI token request sent with `authorization_details` , which contains some "mandatory" claims, Keycloak properly enforces that the claim is really mandatory.

For example in case of request like:
```
[{"type":"openid_credential","credential_configuration_id":"sd-jwt-credential-config-id","locations":["https://localhost:8543/auth/realms/test"],"claims":[{"path":["lastName"],"mandatory":true}]}]
```
When such request is used, but user doesn't have `lastName` (and hence `lastName` claim missing from the credential), then the OID4VCI credential request fails. This is expected IMO.

However when the claim is configured as mandatory by OID4VCI protocol mapper (and subsequently announced as mandatory in the OID4VCI metadata well-known response for the particular credential), then it is not enforced that the claim is present and credential-response is successfully issued even without that mandatory claim -> KO

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-10)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The credential-response is issued just if mandatory claims enforced in both `authorization_details` parameter as well as in protocol mappers (and subsequently OID4VC metadata) are present

### Actual behavior

The credential-response is issued even if mandatory claims configured by protocol mappers (and subsequently OID4VC metadata) are not present

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

The related code is `OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.validateRequestedClaimsArePresent` , but checks just the mandatory claims requested in the `authorization_details`, but not by the config.

It worth to mention that there are multiple other issues with the "mandatory" claims, which maybe worth to be fixed.


- **Issue 2:** Mandatory claims seems to be enforced just when credentialScopeId is the same as name of the `scope` .
The `OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.validateRequestedClaimsArePresent` uses `String claimsKey = AUTHORIZATION_DETAILS_CLAIMS_PREFIX + scope;` . However the `OID4VCAuthorizationDetailsProcessor.buildAuthorizationDetailResponse` uses `String claimsKey = OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.AUTHORIZATION_DETAILS_CLAIMS_PREFIX + credentialConfigurationId;`

- **Issue 3:** - Even claims configured as mandatory are probably not enforced right now. There is `BadRequestException` correctly thrown [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/921b10ee804224ff60396c0c70af5f97b612ba10/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oid4vc/issuance/OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java#L1472) , but that Exception is caught [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/921b10ee804224ff60396c0c70af5f97b612ba10/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oid4vc/issuance/OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java#L1479) causing the flow to successfully continue even without mandatory claims (Just some errors in the server.log are added).

- **Issue 4:** Assuming that there is subsequent OAuth2 login request for `authorization_code` grant (Regular login request, not any use of OID4VCI expected), which happens in the same browser session where some other OAuth2 login request with some OID4VCI `authorization_details` previously happened. Then the `authorization_details` is still returned in the token-response and it seems that credential-offer is also created (not 100% sure. To be doublechecked...). 
The subsequent login requests should not return `authorization_details` and should not create any credential offers.
ATM It seems that saving authorization_details as a note to the userSession may not be the best approach. Might need to double-check, but probably worth to store them to the token instead (maybe both access-token and refresh-token) instead of storing as a note inside userSession. I've created dedicated issue for this one: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44961
commit_message:
Mandatory claims are not enforced for OID4VCI
closes #44796

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: Notification_id returned from the credential response
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

There is claim `notification_id` returned from the successful credential-response. However Keycloak doesn't have any support for further receiving of notifications (no support for `notification_endpoint` described in the [OID4VCI specification](https://openid.net/specs/openid-4-verifiable-credential-issuance-1_0.html#name-notification-endpoint) and this claim is not used anywhere for anything .

Returning `notification_id`, which is not supported anywhere seems to be confusing. Would be probably good to remove the `notification_id` from the credential response (preferred way ATM) or add proper support for the `notification_id` (would be probably more work and increases scope of OID4VCI feature and hence not preferred IMO).

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-10)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Not returning `notification_id` from the credential-response. It is confusing to return it considering that Keycloak doesn't support it further.

### Actual behavior

The `notification_id` is returned

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

_No response_

refactor(oid4vc): remove notification ID handling and related endpoint (#44844)

issue_title: Keycloak retains mapped firstName value and does not nullify it when upstream identity provider stops sending the claim
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

In a realm configured with Identity Brokering, when a user authenticates through an upstream Identity Provider (IdP), Keycloak correctly maps incoming claims such as firstName to the local Keycloak user.

However, if the upstream IdP later stops sending the `firstName` claim, Keycloak does not clear or overwrite the previously stored value in the local user. Instead, Keycloak retains the old value indefinitely.

This results in stale or outdated user profile information and inconsistent synchronization with the upstream IdP.

### Version

26.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When the upstream IdP no longer includes a previously mapped claim `firstName`, Keycloak should set it to null or overwrite it with an empty value if configured.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak retains the old attribute value even when the incoming IdP assertion does not contain the claim `firstName`. There is no built-in mapper option to clear the attribute when it disappears from the IdP response.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure an Identity Provider (e.g., OIDC/SAML) in a Keycloak realm.
2. Create a user in the upstream IdP and include the firstName claim.
3. Configure a mapper in Keycloak to map the IdP firstName claim to the Keycloak user’s firstName attribute.
4. Authenticate — Keycloak correctly stores the received value.
5. Update the user in the upstream IdP to remove the firstName claim.
6. Authenticate again.
7. Observe that the Keycloak user still has the old firstName value.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Test framework supplier dependencies broken
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

Currently a supplier gets a dependency from the registry while it is being created. This works fine as long as the dependency is requested by the test, but if it is not then the registry will just look up whatever exists in the registry. This can lead to a non-compatible dependency being used.

An example of this is found in ManagedCertificates and AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier. If one test runs with a custom ManagedCertificates configuration, this is left running after the test. Now when the AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier looks up the dependency it just re-uses this instance, instead of a new one with the default config.

A workaround was introduced while adding mTLS support to ManagedCertificates, but this is only for the above situation. In general dependencies must be declared upfront by suppliers so the registry can check for compatibility. This requires changing how dependencies are retrieved by all suppliers, which was out of scope for that PR.

This issue is a follow-up to update all suppliers to declare dependencies up-front, and refactor registry to not allow getting dependencies that have not been declared already.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Dependencies are re-created as needed

### Actual behavior

Can attach to non-compatible dependency

### How to Reproduce?

Remove getDependencies implementation in AbstractKeycloakServerSupplier

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Consider this scenario:

- OIDC Authentication login request sent with some scope corresponding to OID4VCI (EG. `scope=oid4vc_natural_person`) and with the `authorization_details` parameter asking for creating credential-offer of some OID4VCI credential
- Credential-offer is created and `authorization_details` are added to the token response -> OK
- Now assume that there is 2nd OIDC authentication login request to the same client in the same browser. This is normal SSO login request, which does not do anything related to OID4VCI . However in the token-response, the `authorization_details` are still returned after this 2nd OIDC authentication flow is finished  -> KO
- Also I think that the access-token returned from this 2nd-request is possible to be used in the credential-request as that 2nd request created OID4VCI credential-offer -> KO

### Version

nightly (from 2025-12-17)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only the 1st token-response, which was triggered from the OIDC flow asking for OID4VCI credential, is supposed to return `authorization_details` and create credential-offer. The subsequent OIDC login requests in same browser session should not do that (especially if those requests are regular SSO login requests not asking for any OID4VCI scope or authorization_details)

### Actual behavior

The subsequent token-responses (2nd and others) are adding `authorization_details` even if they should not

### How to Reproduce?

See details

### Anything else?

I think we can add `authorization_details` as a claim into access-token (and maybe also probably refresh-token if issued) instead of adding them as a note in userSession. Storing them inside token instead of userSession can make sure that credential-request can be successful just if the sent token contains the relevant `authorization_details` . That should also help with fixing the issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44670 and probably also https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/44534 as userSession may not be needed anymore when pre-authorization flow is used.

issue_title: Default redirect URI for an organization should be the Home URL of the account client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Follow up of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/33201

When an organization’s redirect URL is left empty, Keycloak currently defaults to the account console URL. This ignores the `account` client’s configured Home URL (base URL). As a result, installations that customize the `account` client base URL will make users redirected to the wrong location after organization invitation is completed.

### Version

26.4.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If the organization redirect URL is blank, Keycloak should resolve and use the account client’s base URL (including root URL resolution), and only fall back to the account console URL if the account client is missing or has no base URL set.

### Actual behavior

`account` client’s configured Home URL (base URL) is ignored and redirected to the wrong location after organization registration or invitation acceptance.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an organization and leave the redirect URL blank.
2. Send an Invitation to the user for the organization.
3. The user completes the Invitation process and then redirected to the account console even if `account` client's Home URL is customized

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix organization invitation redirect to respect account client base URL

When an organization's redirect URL is left empty, Keycloak currently defaults
to the account console URL, ignoring the account client's configured Home URL
(base URL). This fix checks the account client's base URL before falling back
to the default account console URL.

Changes:
- Added resolveAccountClientBaseUrl() helper method in OrganizationInvitationResource
- Added setBaseUrl() method to ClientAttributeUpdater test utility
- Added integration tests for the new behavior

Closes #45052

Signed-off-by: Rathan Naik <30756840+Rathan-Naik@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Client deletion timeout due to large number of client roles
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We are seeing increased timeouts since upgrading from 25.0.6 to 26.4.0 when calling delete on clients that have ~30 client roles attached. This is happening only in an environment with a large number of clients and users, so it seems the latency increase scales with database size: pushing latencies over the timeout window for large environments. We are seeing large numbers of DB queries issued from this operation, with pretty substantial time gaps between some of them (probably ispn syncing across our distributed nodes, though we haven't gotten trace data for that).

We tracked down the source of the issue to the [removeRole](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1e48f9e512feb4795fdbba23910b3288692e64e7/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/JpaRealmProvider.java#L456) method, which changed in the upgrade.

### Version

26.4.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Client deletion does not timeout in large environments

### Actual behavior

Client deletion times out in large environments

### How to Reproduce?

Given a telemetry agent (like datadog) is connected:
1. Run keycloak version 25
2. Delete a client
3. Run keycloak version 26
4. Delete an identical client
5. Observe a larger number of DB queries in version 26


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Optimize deletion of composite roles

Closes #45065

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <alexander.schwartz@ibm.com>
